programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: When Dr. Indiana Jones – the tweed-suited professor who just happens to be a celebrated archaeologist – is hired by the government to locate the legendary Ark of the Covenant, he finds himself up against the entire Nazi regime.

programme.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
programme.desc: An apocalyptic story set in the furthest reaches of our planet, in a stark desert landscape where humanity is broken, and most everyone is crazed fighting for the necessities of life. Within this world exist two rebels on the run who just might be able to restore order.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection
programme.desc: Arthur Bishop thought he had put his murderous past behind him when his most formidable foe kidnaps the love of his life. Now he is forced to travel the globe to complete three impossible assassinations, and do what he does best, make them look like accidents.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: An art collector appeals to Indiana Jones to embark on a search for the Holy Grail. He learns that another archaeologist has disappeared while searching for the precious goblet, and the missing man is his own father, Dr. Henry Jones. The artifact is much harder to find than they expected, and its powers are too much for those impure of heart.

programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
WCW Main Event - 6x20 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x21 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x22 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x23 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x24 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x25 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x26 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x27 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x28 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x29 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x30 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x31 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x32 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x33 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x34 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x35 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x36 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x37 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x38 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x39 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x40 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x41 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x43 - WCW Main Event
Columbo
programme.desc: An old lady who writes popular mystery novels strongly suspects that her niece's husband murdered her, so she exacts revenge by asphyxiating him in her house safe while devising a solid alibi. It's up to Columbo to unravel the subterfuge.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter has a very strange dream involving Arthur.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Mrs. Naugatuck's last-minute demand could jeapordize her wedding.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy gets wrapped up in "Beatlemania" and begins dating a Beatle impersonator. Meanwhile, Blanche begins to worry about her looks after she pulls out all the stops to get a certain man into bed, but to no avail.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy is all excited when she learns that "Jeopardy" is having tryouts in Miami. This leads her to begin cramming for the audition test and later proves to be an annoyance to everyone at the audition.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: Ebbtide VI: The Wrath of Stan
programme.desc: It is the annual Buy Sophia New Shoes day, and Dorothy is forced to take her. Blanche also tags along, mentioning that it might be fun. Meanwhile, Uncle Angelo drops by and Rose is there. Since she is trying to find a good story to report on, she is more than happy to hear Angelo's story. Apparently, his apartment building is infested with huge bugs. When the ladies watch the news, Rose insists they watch the news story about the bugs. Dorothy then tells Rose she and Stan are the landlords. Rose then tells her she didn't realize there was a family side to the story. She won't give up on the story, and a minute later, the cops show up to arrest Dorothy. They have a trial, and Stan drops his attorney for a younger woman who knows nothing. The defense lawyer is unbeatable by giving compliments to the witnesses which makes them vulnerable to his advantage. Stan and Dorothy end up losing and are forced to live in a sleasy apartment building for a while.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: Journey to the Center of Attention
programme.desc: Blanche brings a lonely, Dorothy down to the Rusty Anchor for a night of excitement. However, Blanche, had no intention of being pushed off her thrown by Dorothy, who begins singing and instantly becomes the center of attention.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Sophia and Oliver joy ride in a guest's car and wind up in police custody.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred does not feel like paying his bills and he throws them in the trash claiming that they never arrived, dragging out the services for as long as he can. After doing so, Fred receives threats that say that they will turn off the gas, electricity, and other utilities, and repossess his furniture if he does not pay.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie narrowly escapes a potentially deadly accident at the dock, prompting him to see the light. But can he deal with the demands of Christianity, especially after he is asked to drive a church bus on the same day he was looking forward to going to a New York Jets game?

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.sub-title: George and Archie Make a Deal
programme.desc: George Jefferson seeks Archie's help when he runs for local political office.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Irene and the Jeffersons bail the Bunkers out after Archie buys two thousand dollars worth of aluminum siding from a fast-talking salesman.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Russ & Rekha vs. Cheryl & Karen vs. Susan & Nikki

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Leann & Erin vs. Carin & Maureen vs. Maria & Gary

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in WW II, the stooges are the only survivors of an American ship sunk by an enemy torpedo. Adrift on a raft, they come upon a German battleship and by various means, such as Moe disguising himself as Hitler, and Curly and Larry as Goering and Goebbels, manage to capture the enemy ship.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are engaged to the three daughters of a prison warden. When they learn that some crooks have taken over the prison and their prospective father-in-law has been locked up, they decide to go undercover to rescue him. The stooges sneak into the prison where they find a casino with a fancy party in progress. After swiping some formal attire, they crash the party and get candid camera evidence to expose the crooked goings-on. With the crooks behind bars once again, the stooges are able to get married and all ends well

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are the trainers of "Chopper", a beefy boxer, and they bet their bankroll on Chopper to win his next fight. When "Big Mike", their boss, tells them to have Chopper lose or they'll lose their lives, the boys try to soften up Chopper so he'll lose. The fight gets canceled and the stooges have to contend with an angry Big Mike and his goons.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are beer delivery men who crash the company golf tournament.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are mistaken for studio execs on a Hollywood lot.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges have lost their jobs. Adding insult to injury, they received a letter from Dad with the news that he requires surgery. To help pay for the operation, the father suggests the boys search for uranium on his mining property.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges trek to Mexico to perform in a bullfight, with Joe as the matador and Moe and Larry dressed in a bull costume.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.sub-title: A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge
programme.desc: Jesse Walsh (Mark Patton) moves with his family into the home of the lone survivor from a series of attacks by dream-stalking monster Freddy Krueger (Robert Englund). There, Jesse is bedeviled by nightmares and inexplicably violent impulses. It turns out Freddy needs a host body to carry out his gruesome vendetta against the youth of Springwood, Ohio. While Freddy gains influence, Jesse and his girlfriend, Lisa (Kim Myers), race against the clock trying to figure out what's going on. Note: This episode of Svengoolie was never shown on Me-TV.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne lands a job at a chicken restaurant, with a total dweeb for a supervisor. He wants her to work weekends, which she is unable to do, so she invites the boy to dinner and gets Dan to help him assemble his carburetor for school. But will the kindness pay off in the end?

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: With the adults away, Becky and her friend Dana, decide to really live it up. Using the alcoholic beverages in the house, Becky creates two "tornados," and the girls experience next-day hangovers, which can be the side effects from taking that first sip of a near-fatal glass of beer.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: When Roseanne is called away, Jackie impresses Dan and the kids with her homemaking skills.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan get a blast from the blast when old pal Ziggy cruises into Lanford on a motorcycle, prompting the Conners to resurrect their old bike.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne applies for a job at a beauty salon doing the same domestic chores that she does at home.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan is persuaded to join Roseanne in a weight-loss program that involves exercise.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne doesn't share Jackie's enthusiasm for the exciting new man in her life.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's meddling results in silence between the sisters, but Jackie has exciting news to share.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The Ladies Department is temporarily closed due to refurbishing, so Mrs. Slocombe and Miss Brahms are given counter space over in the Gents. However, the men aren't very happy at having to share their area and do their best to make them unwelcome.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: When the suggestion box provides little in the way of suggestions, the staff decide to play background music and a recorded voice to boost sales. Then, when they learn that a cash prize will be awarded to whomever is the voice, the whole floor offer their voices for the department, but it's up to Mr.Grace to decide.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony have a discussion about common misconceptions caused by cartoons.
Featured cartoons:
14 Carrot Rabbit (1952) Pet Peeve (1954) All Fowled Up (1955) Fleets of Stren'th (1942) Wild Wild World (1960) Hip Hip-Hurry! (1958)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: One member of a mystery-writing-team decides to kill his more talented partner when the better writer decides to go solo.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to prove their cleaning skills to Sophie so she will hire them for her maid service.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie's (Jennifer Coolidge) beauty overwhelms Earl, Caroline and Max wind up spending Valentine's Day at the hospital; Caroline tries to rekindle an old flame.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have to make kosher cupcakes when they get a gig at a bar mitzvah.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: As a result of his recent assault at the hands of The Brooklyn Brawler, Gorilla Monsoon's working relationship with Bobby 'The Brain' Heenan ends up on shaky ground. Plus, WWE Champion Macho Man Randy Savage takes on Akeem in the featured bout, The Brain Busters face The Young Stallions, and much more on this edition of Prime Time Wrestling!

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob and his family are forced to spend the weekend at Mort the Mortician's while the restaurant is being de-molded. Linda and Bob double date with Mort and a female mortician he meets online while Tina babysits Gene and Louise in Mort’s crematorium.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra discovers that her mother-in-law, Marie, likes Robert's girlfriend, Amy, better than than her, because Amy is a "good girl."

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra brings pretzels to Ally's T-ball game, and when the person in charge of snacks tells her that pretzels aren't on the approved list, Ray doesn't stand up for her, prominently displaying his fear of being disliked by anyone.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie is sad because she has no friends to hang out with her. Doug decides to introduce Carrie to a woman who just moved into the neighborhood. Carrie spends a lot of time with her new friend which makes Doug jealous.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug Heffernan (played by Kevin James) is goofing off with Deacon Palmer (played by Victor Williams) at work, and suddenly ends up with a staple in his "area". Hilarity ensues when he then has to suffer through the pain while at a meeting with a banker because of an argument with his wife Carrie (played by Leah Remini).

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer finds Jerry's old birthday checks from his grandmother and insists he deposits them, which puts Nana in unexpected debt. Elaine accidentally tells the high talker a secret. Mr Pitt eats a Snickers bar with utensils.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George's telephone line crosses with that of a woman named Donna Chang, and everyone seeks her advice when she convinces them that she's Chinese. Jerry winds up dating her. Things get worse when Frank and Estelle announce a separation. Jerry and Elaine can't seem to figure out why Frank Costanza is having mysterious business dealings with a man in a cape.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene wants nothing to do with David after Molly kisses him; and a new friend puts a smile on Bev's face.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene springs a prom-night surprise on David: she has rented a motel room. Meanwhile, Dan is reluctant to share with Roseanne his plans for a new business.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael was shocked when he received a call from Corporate wanting him to interview for a position. Michael was happy, but he also had other things on his mind. Beach Day was coming up and he was taking the crew out for some fun in the sun. Not before reminding Toby he couldn't go because somebody had to stay in the office.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At Marge’s insistence, Bart has a “play date” with Ralph. Bart suddenly sees the upside of this friendship when he discovers Chief Wiggum’s “master key” and goes to explore Springfield’s abandoned prison.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart has his ear pierced and gets an ear-ring but Homer confiscates it.Later Homer and some colleagues join the Naval Reserve and sail on a submarine,where Homer accidentally fires the captain out of the torpedo hatch and,left in charge,allows the sub to drift inti enemy waters. It is attacked and springs a leak but Homer saves the day by sealing it with Bart's ear-ring before returning home for a dishonorable discharge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Facing an IRS audit, Homer agrees to help the feds bust Mr. Burns for a missing trillion-dollar bill. When Burns makes an anti-government stand, Homer flees with him to Cuba.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During a prank that goes wrong Bart destroys Groundskeeper Willie's shack. At the same time lazy Homer is getting a helper monkey called Mojo to do his chores and Lisa is the anchor on a children's T.V. news show. When Bart joins her as co-presenter and they start to compete for stories she takes her revenge by sending him to the scrapyard where Willie is sleeping rough.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer feuds with the Springfield garbage men and decides to run for Sanitation Commissioner. After winning the election with a sleazy campaign, Homer can’t keep the extravagant promises he made to the town.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly has to tap dance at a dance recital because of a prank she pulled.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: A Three Job, No Income Family
programme.desc: Al finally convinces Peggy to contribute to the household income but feels emasculated when Peggy starts bringing home huge "commish" checks from her cosmetics sales job, earning more money than Al, himself. Even Al's new part-time job as a crew member at Burger Trek can't put him back in the lead as family breadwinner. Things go from bad to worse when Al finds the book containing the names of Peggy's customers.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: While driving Peggy back from the video store, Steve gets cut off in traffic and Peggy gives the driver "the finger". Steve then asks the Bundys to come over to his and Marcy's house to watch some videos, as protection when he's afraid of a violent reprisal when he is followed home by the man. When there's a knock on the front door, Al convinces Steve to take the first punch... and Steve ends up knocking down a midget.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Steve and Marcy ask Peggy to look over their house, but when a moving company asks for "Steves Roadhouse", Peggy allows the company to tow away the Rhoades' home, and they have to spend a night at the Bundys - where Kelly is having a slumber party.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Peggy go to their high school reunion. Peggy wants to be prom queen and she spent a lot of money calling old friends and buying new clothes to gain votes. But when she thinks she has the majority of the votes, her rival Connie Bender appears and surprises Peg with the fact that Connie is now in the lead to be prom queen.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Married... with Prom Queen: The Sequel (2)
programme.desc: Peggy tries desperately to get more votes but it seems that most of the people are going to vote for Connie. Bud and Kelly ask Marcy and Steve about the sixties and after some history, Bud and Kelly dress up as hippies and visit the prom. Al runs into his old rival Jack, who appears to be Connie's husband, and Al and Jack decides to fight outside. Meanwhile, Peg rigs the election ... will she win?

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After his two best friends find dates for themselves, Bud is desperately seeking any girl who will date him, so he resorts to getting a life-sized mannequin to pose as his date for the evening. Meanwhile, Al comes up with a million-dollar invention for a shoe salesmen's convention called "Shoe Lights". But they're pretty pathetic; he just takes the headlights and battery out of the car and straps them onto Kelly's shoes.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: It's the 35th anniversary of Sanford & Son Salvage and Lamont has two gifts planned to give Fred. The first is a new window with the specially printed title of their business and the other is an expensive ring that everyone has chipped in to buy. However, when Fred runs across it, he becomes convinced that it was stolen from Frank Sinatra, after Frank's hotel room was ransacked by the Tip-Toe Bandit.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Grady is bursting with news and breaks it to Fred and Lamont that he's engaged to a woman he met at J.C. Penny. However, Fred and Lamont are shocked to discover the "raging beauty" Grady raved about is not exactly a beauty queen. Fred soon finds Grady spending all his time with her and sets a plan into motion to break them up.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's friends Julio and Ah Chew are getting on Fred's nerves and Lamont takes on the challenge of trying to change Fred's bigotry towards minorities. When Lamont fails, he decides to move out and in with Julio, something that unnerves Fred who doesn't seem to get use to the arrangement.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The tax season has come and after an accountant goes through the books he finds that Fred and Lamont will end up owing the government. The accountant explains the situation and Lamont decides to go out and gets a job at a clothing store, so more money is coming into the household. Fred fears this may be the end of Sanford & Son Salvage.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris finds proof that makes him think he is adopted, so he sets off to find his natural parents, an Amish couple.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris goes to work for the police in a sting operation after he buys a counterfeit watch, the Chrono-Sync 2000.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky returns peoples dropped items to get enough money for an ice cream soda.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and his pet ostrich, Lulu, get invited to perform on Broadway for $75/week.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky is in the foreign legion.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: At race day, Porky finds himself up against famous stars such as Laurel and Hardy, and Charlie Chaplin.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky has a dream about what it would be like to be married to Petunia.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A typical afternoon at the movies is lampooned in this looney trip to the cinema.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: New duck hunter Porky is constantly taunted by a very early version of Daffy, and all the other ducks.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky Pig and Dirty Digg, two building contractors, are in a war for the deal to build the town hall.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys watch the touching drama ""Joey Buttawipo: My Story;"" Mr. Van Driessen gives them informational books about getting in touch with their feminine sides; they run for Class Treasurer at school.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys head to Maxi-Mart for some Friday night action; they meet a hot biker chick with larceny on her mind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys accidentally score highly on an IQ test and wind up in the gifted class, where they destroy an actual gifted kid's brain.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis, Can You Spare a Dime?
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head get lessons from a panhandler on how to get cash on the streets.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Raccoons steal Beavis and Butt-Head's nachos, and the boys fight back.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Placed in a ""Scared Straight"" program at school, the boys visit a prison and thrash with some inmates who like Judas Priest and Iron Maiden.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut tries to get Beavis and Butt-head to work out.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head learn about evolution and dream of scoring as cavemen.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
An amateur filmmaker documents America's youth culture in a film featuring Beavis and Butt-head.
videos:
The Ramones - "Substitute"
Blur - "Chemical World"
Morphine - "Thursday"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head go tubing on a river and end up lost at sea.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Mrs. Rene stops by to give Pee-wee a letter from a faraway "place" - Japan! It's from Oki Doki, a young Japanese man who soon appears at the Playhouse door. By introducing Pee-wee to sushi, origami, and Gamara, Oki proves that he has a lot to share about his homeland, and Pee-wee has a lot to learn!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Miss Yvonne teaches the gang how to make cheese balls, and Pee-wee makes a mobile. The fun goes "on" and on!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Although Richie is miffed at Tony for forcing him to build a ramp for the pizza-parlor owner he paralyzed, he decides to make a peace offering. Unhappy with their lowly status as Christopher's lackeys, Sean and Matt decide to pledge their allegiance to Richie--through a violent, unexpected act.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Christopher has an out-of-body experience that spooks Paulie to no end. Carmela urges Tony to have a vasectomy. Visiting her own psychiatrist, Melfi reveals the 'unholy alliance' she's made with Tony...as well as her growing dependence on pills and alcohol.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael works with the Joint Council of Afterlife Affairs to smooth out kinks in the system. Jason and Tahani move on. Eleanor tries to influence Chidi.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon and the Sea Snake battle the Crabfeeder. The realm celebrates Aegon's second nameday. Rhaenyra faces the prospect of marriage.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Jon and his band of men go beyond the wall in search of a Wight to capture and return to Kings Landing. Arya and Sansas relationship continues to derail with the help of LittleFinger. And Tyrion and Daenerys discuss the future of her reign.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: When a six-year-old boy is brought in with the same symptoms as another patient who died years ago under House's care, House believes the cases are identical and he can predict the course of the boy's illness. Meanwhile, the clinic hosts a poker night to benefit the oncology department.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A young woman swallows a bottle of sleeping pills - not to kill herself but to go to sleep, something she says she hasn't done in 10 days. Then her case deteriorates, but her partner is in a perfect position to help her. Meanwhile, House keeps falling asleep because living with Wilson has disrupted his sleep pattern. Cameron's mad at Foreman for swiping her ideas for a medical-journal article, and at House for letting him do it.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homer forgets to pick up Bart from soccer practice, Bart joins a mentor program called Big Brothers presenting himself as an orphan, and is assigned a big brother called Tom. When Homer finds out, he joins the program too as a big brother.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Feeling sorry for Ralph, Lisa sends him a Valentine card. Having developed a crush on her, Ralph invites Lisa to go see the Krusty the Clown show. She accepts but after Krusty asks them if they are boyfriend and girlfriend, Lisa lets Ralph know that she just wants them to be friends.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a DUI, Homer swears off beer for a month. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa's sibling rivalry plays out in the school science fair.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr. Burns revokes the dental plan from the power plant's union contract, and when Homer realizes that he'll now have to pay for Lisa's new braces, he leads the power plant employees on a strike, while Mr. Burns struggles to put an end to the rioting.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So It's Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: An April Fools' Day prank provides the jumping-off point for Simpson family flashbacks.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After watching a particularly tame Itchy and Scratchy show Lisa and Bart send one of their own scripts to the studio but it is rejected as they under age so they write some more and get Grandpa to say that he is the author. As a result Grandpa wins an award but at the presentation ceremony is horrified to see one of the cartoons for the first time. Homer,meanwhile,has to attend night school after it is discovered that he never actually graduated.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge teaches Bart at home after Skinner expels him. Lisa is horrified to find that it is Whacking Day,when Springfield citizens beat snakes to death so,helped by Barry White,she and Bart rescue the snakes by directing them to the Simpsons' house. Bart discovers that the real origin of Whacking Day was to beat up Irishman,a fact which impresses Skinner who lets him back in the school.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by her family's demands during a flu epidemic, Marge accidentally shoplifts at the Kwik-E-Mart and winds up in prison.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty's Hollywood pals help when his show is canceled after a new star causes his ratings to plummet.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer recalls when he, Apu, Barney and Principal Skinner became recording icons.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David’s photographer ex-boyfriend arrives in town to do a photo series on Moira. Johnny and Roland suspect that Bob is cheating at poker.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny tries to avoid the spread of lice in the motel. Moira tries to get out of having a flower garden named after her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to bring in extra money to help pay off their bank loan by taking second jobs as a pastry chef and a hostess at a chic restaurant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max pursues Nashit, a handsome new waiter at The High, despite Joe’s “no hook-up” rule.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie takes Max and Caroline shopping for their bridesmaids’ dresses, the girls enlist the diner gang’s help to hide the ugliest ones, in the hopes of avoiding complete and utter humiliation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Look Of The Irish
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to break Nash into the modeling business in an effort to bring in extra money acting as his managers. Also, Sophie and Oleg attempt to keep celibate for 30 days before their wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s attempt to break Nash into the modeling business comes to a screeching halt when his mother arrives from Ireland to retrieve her son.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline help launch the new branch of The High at JFK airport, and Sophie discovers that Oleg may have an ulterior motive for wanting to marry her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are tasked with retrieving Sophie’s wedding dress from customs and safely transporting it to the church in time for her big wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline team up with the diner staff in a frantic attempt to keep their businesses open when the city targets Han's diner and their cupcake window for destruction. Also, Sophie and Oleg announce that they are trying to have a baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the shower at Max and Caroline's apartment breaks, they get part-time jobs at a fancy gym's juice bar and enjoy free use of the locker room. However, their jobs and free showers are jeopardized when Caroline has a run-in with a group of rich moms who are jealous of the attention she's getting from a sexy trainer.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline's ex Andy returns and she is denial over how they broke up. And when he informs her that he's about to get married, she offers to make the wedding cake. And she is constantly wondering if she made a mistake breaking up with him. And she tries to find wrong with him but can't.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's cupcake shop is boycotted after they refuse to sell cupcakes to "I", an artist who wants to put cocktail weenies in them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han insists the diner gang attends a mandatory night out in an "Escape Room Club" when he feels they aren't working together as a team. However, once they're locked inside the "Alice in Wonderland"-themed room, they find Han has an ulterior motive.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline's grandmother Astrid awakens from a coma with no knowledge of her family's massive money scandal, Caroline attempts to keep the truth hidden for a bit longer by giving Astrid one last fancy luncheon.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg's pro-basketball playing cousin comes to town to play a game against Max's favorite team and gifts her with two free tickets, but chaos ensues when the girls interfere with the game and risk ruining their cupcake business' reputation in the process.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Earl is not invited to play with his old jazz band at their reunion show, Max and the diner gang join forces in order to get him back on stage performing.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Colin and Isaac's friendship is tested. Roy is asked to do a press conference.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: While some of the Greyhounds head home to play for their countries in international matches, Edwin Akufo brings a business proposal to Rebecca.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman shows the identifying structure of human fingerprints, and explains why humans fart, what this gaseous emission is made of and why it smells...

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Wacky scientist Beakman and his assistant Josie, and Lester, an actor dressed as a mangy rat, answers an inquiry about where puddles go after it has rained. Although Lester suggests they are licked up by stray dogs, Beakman proceeds to explain the mysteries of rain and evaporation using a variety of household props. In the "Beak-Mania" portion of the show, Beakman attempts to answer, in rapid succession, short viewer inquiries on topics such as living prehistoric animals (the platypus), the most commonly used word in the English language and the heaviest animal in the history of the world (blue whale). Then, after a trick in which he balances a pair of forks atop a toothpick, Beakman forces Josie to reveal the question for the show's final segment, "What is snot?" Turning to volcanoes, Beakman explains the processes in the Earth's interior that create, and then discharge, molten magma. With the aid of film footage of volcanic eruptions, Beakman employs a foam rubber model of the Earth and a rubber glove to show how magma moves from deep inside the Earth and appears on the surface as lava. Finally, asked by Josie about why some volcanoes explode, Beakman uses a cherry pie to demonstrate the power, and mess, of a volcanic eruption.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the laws of gravity; Galileo shows the effects of gravity;. Sir Isaac Newton explains inertia; how a seat belt helps save lives.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.sub-title: Noises at Night, Beakmania & The Nose
programme.desc: Beakman explains why your house creaks at night. And then explains the purpose of snot and mucus in your nose.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains how blood provides food and oxygen to the human body and removes the blood cells' waste products while defending your body from disease. He also covers questions on the speed of the fastest dog and the longest word, an experiment with salt and dreams and your imagination.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: The show is way out there, way far away. After all, it's in Outer Space. When you look at the night sky on a clear dark night, you can see thousands of stars. There are far more than you could count. And, they are way out there. They are very, very far away. It's about the hardest thing to imagine about space. Let's talk about the nearest star to us, the Sun. If somehow outer space were not an icy cold vacuum with nothing to eat, drink, or breathe, and we could drive there in a car, to get to the Sun at freeway speed of 100 kilometers/hour (61 miles/hour), it would take171 years of driving without stopping to sleep or get gas out in the near nothingness of space. That's just to the Sun. To get to even the closest star, Proxima Centauri, would take 40,000 years. On top of that, most things, billions and billions of stars, are much, much farther away than that! It is just astonishing. How do we know that everything is so far away? We have watched the sky for centuries.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Bill Nye's anatomical lecture explains about how eyes operates in our bodies. Anything can be shown right side up, but start out upside down. It is a quick deception our brain performs by the way is the part of our eyes that we can see in physical form.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Richard gives Larry’s hairdresser a bad review for saying he’s bald, he gets offered a free haircut. This inspires Gumball and Darwin to go on a reviewing spree across town, forcing Larry to give them stuff with the threat of a bad review. But, just because the customer is always right doesn’t mean that what they’re doing isn’t wrong.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When a failed test grade brings down Gumball’s grade point average, he is forced to return to kindergarten.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin find out why Richard's diet wasn't working, and after they help him lose weight, he becomes more obnoxious.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball learns that his nemesis, Rob, has found a new nemesis, he tries to sabotage Rob's new "enemy-ship", much to the frustration of Penny.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball refuses to accept he is the only kid at school that does not have special powers. He convinces Mrs. Jötunheim, Hector's mom, to take him on as her apprentice but she soon learns leaving Gumball unsupervised around magic is a big mistake.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The prize jewel on the Secret Joyful Burger menu is a mysterious burger, but to order it you need to know its name. When Larry refuses to tell Richard, Gumball, and Darwin what it is called, they go to super-sized lengths to discover it.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball finds out that Ocho has an uncle named Mario, he becomes desperate to meet him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When they see the school bullies making fun of Sussie, Gumball and Darwin decide to try and help her act less weird. They carefully observe her behavior and decide that the only thing they have to change is absolutely everything.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard discovers that going into a bank whilst wearing a motorcycle helmet can lead to trouble.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: From police chases to the inside scoop on Daisy the Donkey, Channel 6 Elmore News has all the top local stories. Experienced newscaster Kip Schlezinger brings the latest news, sports and weather, even when there is absolutely nothing to report.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The discovery of a baby bird fuels Starfire and Cyborg to discover their inner children have been awakened.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin attempts to make the Teen Titans to be taken more seriously by the real heroes from Young Justice.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans visit Starfire's home world of Tamaran to try and learn what makes her so sweet and loving.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven and Beast Boy are surprised to discover that their exes, Terra and Aqualad, are dating each other.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin insists that he is a 'multiple trick pony.'

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Pizza is the source of the Titans' goofy humor.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Two Bumble Bees and a Wasp
programme.desc: When Robin tries to teach the others to have more respect for money, he ends up learning a lesson.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg is looking forward to a marathon of '80s television shows, but the remote appears to be missing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans immerse themselves into a variety of video game worlds.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven decides to hang out with Rose Wilson because she is cool; the others believe they are lame by comparison so they attend Cool School.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy are about to take that last long walk at the dog pound when a sweet rich old lady falls for them and adopts them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this parody of Pinocchio, Renwaldo, the village of Egg Smithëe, makes a puppet boy name Egg Yölkeo.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren can't stand Stimpy anymore, so he sends Stimpy on a one-way ticket out of town. Then they are hit by a bus and get united (literally) in a cheap hospital.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren hits Stimpy in the nose and he starts to sing with a crooner-like voice. Stimpy becames famous, until his nose leaves him because of being slapped many times.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are going to be the first dog and cat sent up to space in the Russian space program

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Hilarity ensues as some major intestinal cramps convince Beavis that he might be pregnant.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a wedding in hopes that they can make out with the bride.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After watching a horror movie, Beavis and Butt-head run around the neighborhood trying to scare people by screaming at them.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis, Can You Spare a Dime?
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head get lessons from a panhandler on how to get cash on the streets.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head find a dead squirrel and try to sell its fur.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Partridge Family Get Shot will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross' nephew visits the show, but doesn't get along very well with the cast.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The A-Team Makes One Cup of Coffee Last 5 Hours will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The kids hang out - at the arcade, the library, malls and summer camp, after school, at the Burgery, around the set, outside prison, stores, locker and dressing rooms. They also hang laundry, hang tough, and generally just hang in there.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Wizard Shows You How to Make Rain will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair makes a weather machine, but it doesn't work correctly.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Knight Rider Wants a Ferrari will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is jealous of Christine after she gets a raise.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Dunking for Dollars will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The series is sold to various foreign countries. Adam acts as millionaire Ronald Rump.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Love Connection Short Circuits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Doug pretends to be Cupid and tries to fix up the entire cast with a girl/boyfriend.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. T Thinks He's a Girl will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair tries out different jobs to see which one is right for him. Lisa does a paper for school on what people want to do for their careers.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Super Monster Theatre will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
As everyone confesses what he or she is afraid of, the Locker Monster attacks Vanessa.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Family Ties Tied Much Too Tight will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross convinces Alasdair that he may be the descendant of a millionaire. Ross then sells fake family trees to the rest of the cast, showing them as being related to Napoleon.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Wreck of the Love Boat will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa thinks that being optimistic will save her plant from dying.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Shoots the Ayatollah will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decides to get rich by selling Instant Revenge to the cast members. Alasdair decides to do it in cognito as the Masked Revenger.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Sexual Pioneers vs. The World of Violent Films
programme.desc: It's Night stalkers and Silk Stockings, as Violent Movies takes on Sexual Pioneers and Lingerie. It's the classic battle of Scarfacers versus the leather and lacers. Games - Great Holes of Glory, Log Drop, One & One Make #2 & Irritable Bowl Syndrome.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's hard lovin' and hard labor as celebrated chick magnets take on famous felons. It's the classic battle of ladies mens vs. state pens. Games - Cut the Cheese, Sinkers & Floaters, Dead End Zone, Eat Shitake.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's punch lines and bungee cords, as the stars of comedy take on the stars of "Jackass." It's the classic battle of non-stop yuks vs. stapled nuts. Games - Sprayed and Neutered, Boulder Dash, Door Jammed & Rotating Surfboard of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The Young and Rich vs. Men's Magazines
programme.desc: It's idle money and sexy bunnies as the young and rich take on the men's magazine industry. It's the classic battle of spoiled jetsetters vs. Penthouse petters. Games - Rump Bumpers, Hand Job, Dash to Death & Pole Riders.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's constipation and conservation as those with food disorders take on the environmental industry. It's the classic battle of diet pills versus dying seals. Games - Survivor - Seamen Island, Log Drop, Ball Busters & Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Hot Chicks of Primetime vs. Hot Celebrity Moms
programme.desc: It's beautiful babes and baby buggies, as the ladies of primetime TV take on hot celebrity moms. It's the classic battle of collagen lips vs. spreading hips. Games - Pinheads, Sinkers & Floaters, Irritable Bowl Syndrome & Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Workers at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue, Washington, DC 20001 take on the rest of the world on this week's episode of MXC.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: People Who P!$$ Us Off vs. Worst Jobs
programme.desc: It's unemployment lines and over the liners, as the world's worst jobs take on the people we love to hate. It's the battle of unpleasant work vs. unpleasant jerks. Games - Tumbling Dominoes of Doom, Wallbuggers, Elimination Idol & Surfboard of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Most Extreme Elimination Challenge - Top 25 Most Painful Eliminations of Season 2

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: An obstruction by her glasses during a driving lesson causes Daria to contemplate trading them in for contact lenses. The trouble is, she fears that by doing so she will be viewed as being just as vain and superficial as the rest of her vapid fellow classmates.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The new spirit of "volunteerism" is mandatory at Lawndale High. While reading to senior citizens, Daria finds that her unenthusiastic tone makes her just as unpopular at the nursing home as at school.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A fantasy show wherein Daria and Jane have to convince Christmas, Halloween and Guy Fawkes Day to return to Holiday Island.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets roped into organizing the school dance; she fobs the job off on Jane.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The publisher of a "Savvy"-like teen magazine comes to Lawndale to hang out with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is forced to work side by side with Kevin at a mall nut stand.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: All the prodigal Lanes come home at once, forcing Jane to temporarily move in with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake has a heart attack and his mother comes to the Morgendorffer household to take care of him; his heart attack inspires Quinn to study to become a heart doctor; two radio DJs broadcast their show outside Lawndale High.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn ""Ka-zonks"" DM and Penfold into a hallucinatory world in an attempt to drive them crazy.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A massive allergy attack caused by an army of remote controlled pillows is causing havoc across London. Not even Danger Mouse and Penfold are immune to this latest scheme masterminded by Baron Silas Greenback.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Crumhorn invents a chemical that allows him to create land sharks to terrorize London. Things get even more complicated when Penfold gets into the formula.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A giant spaceship is spotted heading for Earth. It's part of the ""Intergalactic 147"" snooker game and it wants to put the Earth into a black hole.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Silas Greenback unleashes robots of destruction of increasing size against Danger Mouse, the greatest guardian of peace and justice in the civilized world. First, a duo of Sniver Snappers are delivered to DM's secret hideout. Next, Penfold is almost stomped by a pair of electronic boots. Finaly, Dangermouse comes face to faceplate with Big Tom, The Mouse Masher.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback plans to take over the world by combining the sound of 10.000 stolen bagpipes he has hold up in Castle McStrangle. Luckily, Dangermouse has a good set of lungs up his chest. That is, of course, as long as he and Penfold survive the attack of a mechanical Loch Ness Monster.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Radio Control Plane - Square On Balloon - Armadillo Patient - Folded Paper Flips - Vectrex Drawing - Oldest Living Thing - Touch - Secret Inks - Fireproof Money

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Plastic Hot Air Balloon - Glue Powered Bugs - Apple Flower - Binary Number System - Lung's Capacity - Unbreakable Container - Spoon Into Glass - Suspended Book
